headline: Were French people told that Chernobyl's cloud stopped at the border? A look back at a the making of a national myth
description: The legend of the radioactive cloud that supposedly never crossed into France has left a lasting mark on the national imagination. Yet that narrative has been rewritten many times over the decades.

headline: Inquiry into French public broadcasting was guided by far-right tactics
Russia, an unreliable ally for Mali's weakened junta
New testimony undermines Sarkozy's defense in Libyan campaign financing case
description: A second document from Claude Guéant, Nicolas Sarkozy's former chief of staff, is set to be read on Wednesday, as part of the appeal trial over alleged Libyan funding for Sarkozy's 2007 presidential election campaign.

headline: Senegal's anti-gay law is a smokescreen for the country's powerless government
description: By criminalizing 'acts against nature,' President Bassirou Diomaye Faye and his prime minister, Ousmane Sonko, hope to distract from mounting social tensions and ongoing economic hardship.

headline: Manon Fleury, Michelin-starred chef: 'I constantly thought about cooking because I was anorexic'
description: A former junior French fencing champion, the chef talks about how her eating disorder led her to gastronomy, and, eventually, a Michelin star.

headline: How Le Monde's London correspondent almost became a Reform UK candidate in Britain's local elections
description: After being approached multiple times, Le Monde's London correspondent recounts the questionable recruitment tactics of the far-right party. Her misadventure reflects Reform UK's efforts to try to take London from Labour on May 7.

Global backslide in women's rights did not begin with Trump
description: On March 9, the US refused to sign off on the conclusions of the UN Commission on the Status of Women, the main global women's rights forum. This was the first time since the forum's creation in 1946, and it reflects the growing threat that conservative ideologies pose to feminist achievements.

headline: Should fiction be faithful to history? Film director Xavier Giannoli and historian Tal Bruttmann discuss
description: Xavier Giannoli's latest film, 'Les Rayons et les Ombres,' is a critical and box office success. It has also sparked questions about its approach to the character of Jean Luchaire, a collaborator during World War II. Can a work of art take liberties with the facts, especially when dealing with such a sensitive period?

headline: Annette Messager: 'Without art, I would have become a very mean woman'
description: 'I wouldn't be where I am today if...' In this series, Le Monde interviews a public figure about a decisive moment in their life.  The internationally renowned visual artist recalls the strange atmosphere of the seaside town of Berck, where she grew up.
